---
title: "Lisbon in February"
description: "February is the best month to visit Lisbon. The light is sharp, the city is quiet, and the pastéis de nata are perfect."
author: "Sophia Jaffer"
date: "2025-01-15"
categories:
  - Portugal
  - Cities
---

## The light

There is a particular quality to the light in Lisbon in February. It is winter light — low and slanted — but the city faces south and west and catches it at angles that seem to ignite the tiles on the buildings. The azulejos that cover so many of the façades go from blue to almost white to a kind of pale fire depending on the hour.

I arrived in the late afternoon, which was the right time. The Alfama was golden. I walked for two hours before I could bring myself to stop somewhere and eat.

## The city in the quiet season

February is, for the purposes of a visitor who wants to understand a place, the right time to be there. The tourist infrastructure is present but not dominant. You can get a table at a tasca without reservation. The trams move at something approaching their intended speed.

Lisbon has changed significantly in the past decade — rents have risen, long-term residents have been displaced, the centre has become in parts a kind of stage set for short-term visitors. All of this is true and worth knowing. But in February, in the early morning, in the neighbourhoods slightly beyond the obvious circuits, you can still find the city as it is rather than as it is performing.

## The pastry

The pastel de nata at the Fábrica de Pastéis de Belém is better than it has any right to be, given its fame. It is eaten standing up, still warm, dusted with cinnamon, with a small strong coffee. This is one of those occasions when the famous thing is genuinely worth the trip.

I ate three.
